creator: thierry@thomas.as
text:
ISO_8859-15, or latin 9, is now an official norme. It should supersede ISO_8859-1 (latin 1) in many applications, since it contains the currency sign of the euro, and some other characters requested by some languages (e.g. the œ in french).
	There is no reason to keep DIS_8859-15, beceause DIS was a transitional designation.
	Perhaps is it not too late to rename this before 4.4 Release, since it is only a minor change?

Fix: 

Rename DIS_8859-15 into ISO_8859-15 in the following files:

/usr/src/share/colldef/de_DE.DIS_8859-15.src
/usr/src/share/colldef/es_ES.DIS_8859-15.src
/usr/src/share/colldef/is_IS.DIS_8859-15.src
/usr/src/share/colldef/la_LN.DIS_8859-15.src
/usr/src/share/colldef/map.DIS_8859-15
/usr/src/share/colldef/sv_SE.DIS_8859-15.src
/usr/src/share/mklocale/la_LN.DIS_8859-15.src

/usr/bin/csh/nls/finnish/Makefile:LL=     fi_FI.DIS_8859-15
/usr/bin/csh/nls/french/Makefile:    fr_BE.DIS_8859-15 fr_CA.DIS_8859-15 fr_CH.DIS_8859-15 \
/usr/bin/csh/nls/french/Makefile:    fr_FR.DIS_8859-15
/usr/bin/csh/nls/german/Makefile:LL=     de_AT.ISO_8859-1 de_CH.ISO_8859-1 de_AT.DIS_8859-15 \
/usr/bin/csh/nls/german/Makefile:    de_CH.DIS_8859-15 de_DE.DIS_8859-15
/usr/bin/csh/nls/italian/Makefile:LL=     it_CH.ISO_8859-1 it_IT.DIS_8859-15 it_CH.DIS_8859-15
/usr/bin/csh/nls/spanish/Makefile:LL=     es_ES.DIS_8859-15
